title: Container Creation, Process Lifecycle, CLI State, and Cleanup · Docker Retrieval
summary: An operations reviewer reconstructs container creation, process lifecycle, cli state, and cleanup from delayed evidence, challenges one retained misconception, and extends the deterministic container lifecycle runbook under a changed constraint. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for deterministic container lifecycle runbook after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish create, run, start, attach, exec, stop, kill, restart, pause, wait, remove, and auto-remove state transitions.
Apply names, environment, working directory, user, entrypoint, command, labels, hosts, and platform overrides with correct precedence.
Choose foreground, detached, interactive, TTY, attach, and log behavior while preserving standard streams and exit evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Docker Cleanup Scope Labels
steps.instruction:
review case — A review operator inherits 6 similarly named containers: one created, one running, one exited with code 137, and one carrying the only writable-layer report. Inspect identity and state, preserve evidence, then perform label-scoped cleanup. Competency probe: Use labels, explicit identifiers, dry evidence, and dependency checks to remove only intended containers and resources.
